results.title: ReturnBet
results.text: ReturnBet The ReturnBet endpoint marks a bet in the Accepted state as returned. This call is used when a Partner needs to return (void) a bet due to a specific reason. Important: Only bets in the Accepted state can be returned using this endpoint. If the bet has already been resulted or its outcome has been determined, the call will be rejected. This allows the Partner to handle exceptional cases where a placed bet must be voided and refunded before it is settled. Request URL Sample Request URL Sample Request Body Request Body Response Response http://hostname/api/LangId/PartnerId/PartnerAPI/ReturnBet Method: POST .../api/en/290/Bet/ReturnBet FilterBetReturnModel Success---------ResponseWrapper { StatusCode=”0”, Data={} } Error-----------ResponseWrapper { StatusCode=errorCode, Data=errorMessage } FilterBetReturnModel Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description BetId long Mandatory Bet Id RequestHash string Mandatory required for partner verification ExternalId long Optional Partner’s unique identifier of the bet, if specified, BetId should be 0.
results.html:
ReturnBet

Important: Only bets in the Accepted state can be returned using this endpoint. If the bet has already been resulted or its outcome has been determined, the call will be rejected.

This allows the Partner to handle exceptional cases where a placed bet must be voided and refunded before it is settled.

results.title: UpdateClient
results.text: UpdateClient The UpdateClient endpoint is called by the Partner when a client’s details have been updated on the Partner’s side and those changes need to be synchronized Feedconstruct. This ensures that the client information remains consistent across both systems. Request URL Sample Request URL Sample Request Body Request Body Response Response http://hostname/api/LangId/PartnerId// Client/UpdateClient Method: POST .../api/en/290/Client/UpdateClient ClientModel object Success---------ResponseWrapper { StatusCode=”0”, Data=ClientModel } Error-----------ResponseWrapper { StatusCode=errorCode, Data=errorMessage } ClientModel Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description id int Mandatory Client Id ExternalId string Mandatory, in case the Client ID is not provided Either id or ExternalId must be provided RequestHash string Mandatory required for partner verification FirstName string Optional LastName string Optional MiddleName string Optional Login string Optional username of client in website IBAN string Optional international bank account number of client RegionCode string Optional ISO ALPHA-2 Code of country (FR, GB, RU) Gender int Optional Male = 1, Female = 2 ProfileId int Optional DocNumber string Optional Passport Number of client PersonalId string Optional Unique identity number of client Address string Optional Email string Optional Language string Optional preferred language of client, ISO 639-1 codes Phone string Optional MobilePhone string Optional BirthDateStamp long Optional UNIX timestamp representation of registration date City string Optional City where client lives PromoCode string Optional Promotional code by which client was registered TimeZone decimal Optional Timezone of client (in hours) IsLocked bool Optional Indicates whether the account is locked (true = locked, false = active). CreatedStamp long Optional ModifiedStamp long Optional DocIssuedBy string Optional LastLoginIp string Optional LastLoginTimeStamp long Optional PreMatchSelectionLimit decimal Optional LiveSelectionLimit decimal Optional IsVerified bool Optional SportsbookProfileId int Optional Identifier for the user’s assigned sportsbook profile. GlobalLiveDelay int Optional IsTest bool Optional Mark if the account is a test account CanBet bool Optional Determines if the user is allowed to place bets (true = allowed, false = restricted).

results.title: GetClientDetails
results.text: GetClientDetails The GetClientDetails method is called by FeedConstruct when the Partner calls either CreateBet or GetMaxBetAmount. It uses the AuthToken generated by the Partner and returns the player`s data if the token is valid and the player is logged in to the Partner’s website. If the player is not logged in, the method must return ErrorCode 1008. For other issues—such as an expired or invalid token—relevant error codes should be returned, as specified in the Error Codes section. Important:The client`s currency must match one of the supported values listed in the Currency Codes section. Important:The client`s currency should remain unchanged throughout the client’s lifecycle. URL: /GetClientDetails Example: http://partnerhostname:port/api/GetClientDetails Where http://partnerhostname:port/api is the Partner’s Base URL Method: POST Request parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description AuthToken String Mandatory See AuthToken for details TS Long Mandatory See TS parameter for details Hash String Mandatory See Hash parameter for details Response parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description Login String Mandatory, max length - 255 Client’s Login on the Partner’s side. The value of the ExternalID can be used instead CurrencyId String Mandatory, max length - 3 User’s currency code. ExternalId String Mandatory, max length - 255 Unique ID of the Client in the Partner’s Backend LanguageId String Optional, max length - 2 Two letter code of the Client’s language. For example “en” – for English Email String Optional, max length - 255 Email address of the Client. BirthDate Date Optional Birth date of the Client. Gender Int Optional 1 – for Male, 2 – for Female. CurrentIp String Optional, max length - 50 IP address of the client currently logged in to the Partner’s website. Phone String Optional, max length - 255 Primary phone number of the Client ErrorCode String Optional See Error Codes for valid values. Mandatory in case of error. ErrorText String Optional Error description CountryId String Optional, max length - 2 ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code of the registration country PartnerFlag String Optional, max length - 50 Optional flag field to differentiate some clients from others SportsProfileID Int Optional Identifier for the user’s assigned sportsbook profile. RMTblocked Bool Optional Indicates if Risk Management Tool (RMT) is blocked for this user (true = blocked, false = allowed). Note: Optional fields can be omitted from the response. However, the fields Gender, BirthDate, CurrentIp, Phone, and Email are used for advanced player risk management (e.g., detecting multi-accounting or linked accounts, assigning additional categories, etc.) in the Dedicated package and are recommended to be included in the response if available.
results.html:
GetClientDetails

The GetClientDetails method is called by FeedConstruct when the Partner calls either CreateBet or GetMaxBetAmount.

It uses the AuthToken generated by the Partner and returns the player`s data if the token is valid and the player is logged in to the Partner’s website.

If the player is not logged in, the method must return ErrorCode 1008. For other issues—such as an expired or invalid token—relevant error codes should be returned, as specified in the Error Codes section.

Important:The client`s currency must match one of the supported values listed in the Currency Codes section.

Important:The client`s currency should remain unchanged throughout the client’s lifecycle.

results.title: BetPlaced
results.text: BetPlaced When a bet passes all validations, FeedConstruct initiates the BetPlaced call to the Partner to request final confirmation. During this stage, the bet remains in a pending state until a response is received from the Partner. The Partner has 5 seconds to respond. If no response is received within this timeframe, FeedConstruct automatically initiates a rollback, and the bet is not processed. This mechanism ensures that only confirmed bets are accepted and helps prevent unresolved or stuck bet states due to communication delays. URL: /BetPlaced Example: http://partnerhostname:port/api/BetPlaced Where http://partnerhostname:port/api is the Partner’s Base URL Method: POST Request parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description AuthToken String Mandatory See AuthToken for details TS Long Mandatory See TS parameter for details Hash String Mandatory See Hash parameter for details TransactionId Long Mandatory A unique identifier generated by FeedConstruct for each call related to bets. This is not a bet ID. The Partner should store this ID to detect and prevent duplicate requests. It is also used in the Rollback call to cancel a previously sent transaction. BetId Long Mandatory A unique identifier for the placed bet, generated by FeedConstruct. This ID should be referenced when contacting FeedConstruct Support. Amount Decimal Mandatory The amount of real money, in the Client’s currency, placed on the bet. Created DateTime Mandatory The UTC timestamp indicating when the bet was placed. BetType Int Mandatory 1 – Single/Ordinar, 2 – Multiple/Express, 3 – System, 4 - Chain, 5 - Trixie, 6 - Yankee, 7 - PermedYankee, 8 - SuperYankee, 9 - Heinz, 10 - SuperHeinz, 11 - Goliath, 12 - Patent, 13 - PermedPatent, 14 - Lucky15, 15 - Lucky31, 16 - Lucky63, 60 - SystemCombination. SystemMinCount Int Mandatory in case of BetType=3 (System) Specifies the number of selections in each sub-bet for a System bet. Applicable only when the Bet Type is 3 (System). For example, in a "System 2 of 3" bet, the System Size is 2, meaning each sub-bet includes 2 out of the 3 total selections. TotalPrice Decimal Mandatory The total price/odds/coefficient of the placed bet. Selections Array Mandatory - at least one The list of selections (events) included in the placed bet. Always contains 1 selection for Single bets, and multiple selections for Multiple and System bets. Source Int Optional 42 - Web (default), 4 - Mobile, 16 - Android, 17 - iOS, 98 - Betshop, 99 - Terminal, 6 - New Mobile. The field is not included in hash. OddType int Mandatory The format of odds used by the Client when placing the bet. Supported values: • 0 – Decimal (default and currently the only supported format) • 1 – Fractional • 2 – American • 3 – Hong Kong • 4 – Malay • 5 – Indo ExternalId long Optional The Partner’s ID of the bet. Is sent if received in the initial CreateBet call Selection/Event parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description SelectionId Long Mandatory A unique identifier of the bet’s selection/event in FeedConstruct's backend used to identify the specific selection. SelectionName String Optional, max length - 255 The name of the selection/event. For example, W1 MarketTypeId Long Mandatory A unique identifier of the selection group (market) in FeedConstruct, used to identify the market type of the selection. MarketName String Optional, max length - 255 The name of the market. For example, Match Result. MatchId Int Mandatory A unique ID of the match in FeedConstruct. MatchName String Optional, max length - 500 The name of the match. For example, Barcelona – Real Madrid. MatchStartDate DateTime Optional The UTC timestamp that indicates the start time of the match. EventEndDate DateTime Optional The UTC timestamp that indicates the end of the match. RegionId Int Mandatory A unique identifier of the region the match belongs to. RegionName String Optional, max length - 255 The name of the region. For example, Spain. CompetitionId Int Mandatory A unique identifier of the competition of the match in FeedConstruct. CompetitionName String Optional, max length - 255 The name of the competition. For example, La Liga. SportId Int Mandatory A unique identifier of the sport in FeedConstruct. SportName String Optional, max length - 255 The name of the sport. For example, Football SportFullName String Optional, max length - 500 The full name of the sport. Price Decimal Mandatory The price/odd of the selection. IsLive Boolean Optional True – for a Live match selection, False – for Pre-Match selection. Basis Decimal Optional The handicap value of handicap market types. For example, -1, 2.5, etc MatchInfo String Optional A text describing the score and some live info when the bet is placed on a Live event. IsOutright Boolean Optional True - when a selection is an outright match/market, otherwise - False. HomeTeamName String Optional The name of the home team/player. AwayTeamName String Optional The name of the away team/player. HomeTeamId Int Optional A unique identifier of the home team/player. AwayTeamId Int Optional A unique identifier of the away team/player. Response parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Description Description ErrorCode string See Error Codes for valid values. In case of confirmation the value should be 0. ErrorText String Error description. In case of confirmation it should be empty.
results.html:
BetPlaced

When a bet passes all validations, FeedConstruct initiates the BetPlaced call to the Partner to request final confirmation.

During this stage, the bet remains in a pending state until a response is received from the Partner.

  • The Partner has 5 seconds to respond.
  • If no response is received within this timeframe, FeedConstruct automatically initiates a rollback, and the bet is not processed.

This mechanism ensures that only confirmed bets are accepted and helps prevent unresolved or stuck bet states due to communication delays.

    SystemMinCountIntMandatory in case of BetType=3 (System)Specifies the number of selections in each sub-bet for a System bet. Applicable only when the Bet Type is 3 (System). For example, in a "System 2 of 3" bet, the System Size is 2, meaning each sub-bet includes 2 out of the 3 total selections.

results.title: BetResulted
results.text: BetResulted The BetResulted method is called by FeedCosntruct when an accepted bet is settled, updated, or its result is canceled (i.e., the bet status or winning amount changes). Important: The Partner may receive multiple BetResulted calls for the same bet. Use Cases Examples of when BetResulted may be triggered: Returned Bet: The bet was rejected or voided. → BetState: 2, Amount: BetStakeAmount. Won Bet: The bet won, and the calculated winnings are provided. → BetState: 4, Amount: Winning amount. Lost Bet: The bet lost. → BetState: 3, Amount: 0. Cashed-Out Bet: The bet was cashed out. → BetState: 5, Amount: Cashed-out amount (sent by the partner in the MarkBetAsCashout or CheckAndMarkBetAsCashout requests). Reverted Result:The previously settled result was canceled and the bet was returned to its initial (unsettled) state. → BetState: 1, Amount: 0. Important Notes: Always use the Amount field to determine the net winning or refund for the player. Do not link balance logic to BetState values. BetState is for informational purposes only. If multiple BetResulted calls are received for the same bet: Adjust the Client’s balance by the difference between the new Amount and the previously credited amount. URL: /BetResulted Example: http://partnerhostname:port/api/BetResulted Where http://partnerhostname:port/api is the Partner’s Base URL Method: POST Request parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description AuthToken String Mandatory See AuthToken for details. The AuthToken may be significantly old in this request: For live bets, this may be several hours after placement. For pre-match bets, this may be several days or even weeks later. This delay occurs because some bet outcomes are determined long after the original bet was placed. The Partner must be able to distinguish such delayed transactions and ensure they are still accepted and processed properly. TS Long Mandatory See TS parameter for details Hash String Mandatory See Hash parameter for details TransactionId Long Mandatory A unique identifier generated by FeedConstruct for each call related to bets. This is not a bet ID. The Partner should store this ID to detect and prevent duplicate requests. It is also used in the, Rollback call to cancel a previously sent transaction. BetId Long Mandatory A unique identifier for the placed bet, generated by FeedConstruct. This ID should be referenced when contacting FeedConstruct Support.and other support services at BetConstruct. BetState int Mandatory The state of the bet. • 1 – Accepted (not resulted) • 2 – Returned • 3 – Lost • 4 – Won • 5 – Cashed-out Amount Decimal Mandatory The amount of real money, in the Client’s currency, placed on the bet. He Partner can compare this amount to the previously called BetResulted Amount and increase or decrease the Client’s balance. The Partner can store this value on the bet’s record as the final total amount returned to Client’s balance from this bet. CalcDate DateTime Optional The UTC timestamp of the bet settlement. IsLive Boolean Optional True – for Live selection, False – for Pre-Match selection. BetSelectionState Array of BetSelectionState where SelectionId is Long Optional [{"SelectionId":123,"State":1}] Possible values for a Selection State field are: • 0 - NotResulted • 2 - Returned • 3 - Lost • 4 - Won • 5 - WinReturn • 6 - LossReturn SelectionId will indicate the selection Id within the current bet. IsVoid Boolean Optional True - when the bet is canceled/voided otherwise False IsResettled Boolean Optional True - when the bet has been resettled otherwise False or absent ExternalId long Optional The Partner’s ID of the bet. Is sent if received in the initial CreateBet call VoidReason String Optional Void reason Response parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Description Description ErrorCode string See Error Codes for valid values. Mandatory in case of error. ErrorText String Error description
results.html:
BetResulted

The BetResulted method is called by FeedCosntruct when an accepted bet is settled, updated, or its result is canceled (i.e., the bet status or winning amount changes).

Important: The Partner may receive multiple BetResulted calls for the same bet.

Use Cases

Examples of when BetResulted may be triggered:

  • Returned Bet: The bet was rejected or voided. → BetState: 2, Amount: BetStakeAmount.
  • Won Bet: The bet won, and the calculated winnings are provided. → BetState: 4, Amount: Winning amount.
  • Lost Bet: The bet lost. → BetState: 3, Amount: 0.
  • Cashed-Out Bet: The bet was cashed out. → BetState: 5, Amount: Cashed-out amount (sent by the partner in the MarkBetAsCashout or CheckAndMarkBetAsCashout requests).
  • Reverted Result:The previously settled result was canceled and the bet was returned to its initial (unsettled) state. → BetState: 1, Amount: 0.

Important Notes:

  • Always use the Amount field to determine the net winning or refund for the player.
  • Do not link balance logic to BetState values. BetState is for informational purposes only.
  • If multiple BetResulted calls are received for the same bet:
    • Adjust the Client’s balance by the difference between the new Amount and the previously credited amount.

URL: /BetResulted

Example: http://partnerhostname:port/api/BetResulted

Where http://partnerhostname:port/api is the Partner’s Base URL

Method: POST

  • Request parameters
    • Field Name
    Type
    Requirement
    Description
    AuthTokenStringMandatorySee AuthToken  for details. The AuthToken may be significantly old in this request:
    For live bets, this may be several hours after placement.
    For pre-match bets, this may be several days or even weeks later.
    This delay occurs because some bet outcomes are determined long after the original bet was placed. The Partner must be able to distinguish such delayed transactions and ensure they are still accepted and processed properly.
    TSLongMandatorySee TS parameter  for details
    HashStringMandatorySee Hash parameter  for details
    TransactionIdLongMandatoryA unique identifier generated by FeedConstruct for each call related to bets. This is not a bet ID. The Partner should store this ID to detect and prevent duplicate requests. It is also used in the, Rollback  call to cancel a previously sent transaction.
    BetIdLongMandatoryA unique identifier for the placed bet, generated by FeedConstruct. This ID should be referenced when contacting FeedConstruct Support.and other support services at BetConstruct.
    BetStateintMandatoryThe state of the bet. • 1 – Accepted (not resulted) • 2 – Returned • 3 – Lost • 4 – Won • 5 – Cashed-out
    AmountDecimalMandatoryThe amount of real money, in the Client’s currency, placed on the bet. He Partner can compare this amount to the previously called BetResulted  Amount and increase or decrease the Client’s balance. The Partner can store this value on the bet’s record as the final total amount returned to Client’s balance from this bet.
    CalcDateDateTimeOptionalThe UTC timestamp of the bet settlement.
    IsLiveBooleanOptionalTrue – for Live selection, False – for Pre-Match selection.

    IsVoidBooleanOptionalTrue - when the bet is canceled/voided otherwise False
    IsResettledBooleanOptionalTrue - when the bet has been resettled otherwise False or absent
    ExternalIdlongOptionalThe Partner’s ID of the bet. Is sent if received in the initial CreateBet call
    VoidReasonStringOptionalVoid reason

results.title: BetResulted Retry Logic
results.text: BetResulted Retry Logic When BetResulted call fails due to communication or network, Feedcosntruct tries to resend the message. Each retry is performed after N minutes where N is the retry count. After 5 retries the message remains in the queue with “No Answer” status.
results.html:
BetResulted Retry Logic

When BetResulted call fails due to communication or network, Feedcosntruct tries to resend the message. Each retry is performed after N minutes where N is the retry count. After 5 retries the message remains in the queue with “No Answer” status.

results.title: Rollback
results.text: Rollback The Rollback method is triggered when a bet needs to be canceled due to one of the following reasons: The Partner responds with an error to the BetPlaced call. The Partner does not respond within 5 seconds to the BetPlaced call. This call is sent by FeedConstruct to inform the Partner that the bet should be canceled and not processed further. Handling Duplicate or Repeated Calls If the Partner has not processed the original BetPlaced call with the given TransactionId, this Rollback can be safely ignored. If the Partner has already canceled the transaction, this call can also be safely ignored. Important: In both cases, the Partner must still return a valid success response to prevent FeedConstruct from retrying the call. URL: /Rollback Example: http://partnerhostname:port/api/Rollback Where http://partnerhostname:port/api is the Base URL for all calls. Method: POST Request parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description AuthToken String Mandatory See AuthToken for details. The AuthToken may be significantly old in this request: For live bets, this may be several hours after placement. For pre-match bets, this may be several days or even weeks later. This delay occurs because some bet outcomes are determined long after the original bet was placed. The Partner must be able to distinguish such delayed transactions and ensure they are still accepted and processed properly. TS Long Mandatory See TS parameter for details Hash String Mandatory See Hash parameter for details TransactionId Long Mandatory A unique identifier generated by FeedConstruct for each call related to bets. This is not a bet ID. It is used in the Rollback call to cancel a previously sent transaction. BetId Long Mandatory, but is not used in the Hash Calculation A unique identifier for the placed bet, generated by FeedConstruct. This ID should be referenced when contacting FeedConstruct Support.and other support services at BetConstruct. ExternalId Long Optional The Partner’s ID of the bet. Is sent if received in the initial CreateBet call Response parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Description Description ErrorCode string See Error Codes for valid values. Mandatory in case of error. ErrorText String Error description
results.html:
Rollback

The Rollback method is triggered when a bet needs to be canceled due to one of the following reasons:

  • The Partner responds with an error to the BetPlaced call.
  • The Partner does not respond within 5 seconds to the BetPlaced call.

This call is sent by FeedConstruct to inform the Partner that the bet should be canceled and not processed further.

Handling Duplicate or Repeated Calls

  • If the Partner has not processed the original BetPlaced call with the given TransactionId, this Rollback can be safely ignored.
  • If the Partner has already canceled the transaction, this call can also be safely ignored.

Important: In both cases, the Partner must still return a valid success response to prevent FeedConstruct from retrying the call.

URL: /Rollback

Example: http://partnerhostname:port/api/Rollback

Where http://partnerhostname:port/api is the Base URL for all calls.

Method: POST

  • Request parameters
    • Field Name
    Type
    Requirement
    Description
    AuthTokenStringMandatorySee AuthToken  for details. The AuthToken may be significantly old in this request:
    For live bets, this may be several hours after placement.
    For pre-match bets, this may be several days or even weeks later.
    This delay occurs because some bet outcomes are determined long after the original bet was placed. The Partner must be able to distinguish such delayed transactions and ensure they are still accepted and processed properly.
    TSLongMandatorySee TS parameter  for details
    HashStringMandatorySee Hash parameter  for details
    TransactionIdLongMandatoryA unique identifier generated by FeedConstruct for each call related to bets. This is not a bet ID. It is used in the Rollback call to cancel a previously sent transaction.
    BetIdLongMandatory, but is not used in the Hash CalculationA unique identifier for the placed bet, generated by FeedConstruct. This ID should be referenced when contacting FeedConstruct Support.and other support services at BetConstruct.
    ExternalIdLongOptionalThe Partner’s ID of the bet. Is sent if received in the initial CreateBet call
  • Response parameters

    Type
    Description
    ErrorCodestringSee Error Codes  for valid values. Mandatory in case of error.
    ErrorTextStringError description

results.title: Client

results.title: Bet Selection
results.text: Bet Selection This method pushes settlement outcomes for individual selections in a multiple bet. It is used only for Multiple bet types and only when the settlement does not change the overall bet state. If the bet state changes, the BetResulted method from the Partner API is used instead. Important: The BetSelection method requires subscription; therefore, if the Partner intends to receive these updates, they must inform their project or account manager accordingly. URL: /bet/event Example: http://hostname:port/api/bet/event Method: POST Push Update Parameters Field Name Field Name Type Type Requirement Requirement Description Description betId long Mandatory Bet Id oddId long Mandatory Id of the bet selection (oddId is SelectionId) settlementDate long Mandatory UTC date of settlement status short Mandatory • State of bet event • NotResulted = 0, • Placed = 1, • Returned = 2, • Lost = 3, • Won = 4, • WinReturn = 5 , • LossReturn = 6 externalId long Optional External Id of the bet provided by the partner in the CreateBet call
results.html:

results.title: Bet Limits - Global Limits
results.text: Bet Limits - Global Limits Maximum Single Selection Liability Maximum Single Selection Liability is the maximum net winning amount which any player can win from any single selection. This is the most important indication in our limitation system as all other limits (such as game limit, client limit, market type limit) are percentages of this amount. How the maximum bet amount of a single selection is calculated? The Formula is: Max Single Selection Liability x (Competition or Sport Limit/100) x (Market Type limit/100) x (Client Limit/100) / (Odd -1) Example: Selection liability is set at 15.000 EUR, Competition limit is 10 %, Market type limit is 100%, Client limit is 50%, the selection odd is 1.8. 15.000 x (10 %/100) x (100 % / 100) x (50 % / 100) / (1.8 – 1) = The max bet of the selection of the example mentioned above will be 937,5 EUR After reaching the maximum amount, the player cannot bet any more on the certain selection. The limit can be expired either by betting once the total amount or many times by smaller amounts. Example: Client has placed a bet on Liverpool vs Everton match on the selection W1. All the limits along with the partner selection liability allows to bet on this selection 10,000 EUR. Client can bet on this selection once with max bet (10,000 EUR) and he can place a bet with different amounts, but after reaching 10.000 EUR he can’t place bet on this selection any more until the delay time ends which has been set on global limits (“Reset Player limit after minutes”). It is important to remember that each bet of the player should not be more and less than the minimum and maximum bet stakes amounts in each currency accordingly. Maximum Individual Bet Liability The amount of pure winning (without bet stake) of a single player for one bet. (ex. multiple bet). Maximum Payout Maximum Payout - The maximal amount, which is possible to be won by a single player for one coupon. Maximum Payout is the gross win that a client can receive on a coupon (exception – Super Bets). Note: MaxBet error "ClientBetStakeLimitError" may also happen, when there was a change with Maximum Payout. Important: Limitation changes are not applied immediately, usually it’s needed some time (up to an hour) for the change to affect. Bet Stake per Currency The Currency added in default is EUR. The Partner is allowed to request adding or removing currencies from their project․ Each currency should have the minimum and maximum allowed bet stake amount, which is also mentioned by the Partner. Min Stake Min Stake – the minimal stake for one bet for pre-match or live events. The client will not be able to place a bet with fewer amount than the value, which is set as Min Stake. Pre-Match Max Stake PreMatch Max Bet Stake - maximal stake for one bet for pre-match event. This setting refers to one coupon. The client can place as many Max Stakes, as the Selection Liability (Limit) allows. After reaching the limit, the client cannot place bet on this selection any more until the delay time ends. Live Max Stake Live Max Bet Stake - maximal stake for one bet for live event. This setting refers to one coupon. The client can place as many Max Stakes, as the Selection Liability (Limit) allows. After reaching the limit, the client cannot place bet on this selection any more until the delay time ends. Note: During the integration phase, Partners are subject to a pre-set Turnover Limit for their potential bets. If a Partner receives a "PartnerLimitError" rejection message while making testing bets, it indicates this limit has been reached. Partners cannot increase this Turnover Limit themselves and must contact the support team for assistance.
results.html:
Bet Limits - Global Limits

  • Maximum Single Selection Liability

    • Maximum Single Selection Liability is the maximum net winning amount which any player can win from any single selection. This is the most important indication in our limitation system as all other limits (such as game limit, client limit, market type limit) are percentages of this amount.

    How the maximum bet amount of a single selection is calculated?

    The Formula is:

    Max Single Selection Liability x (Competition or Sport Limit/100) x (Market Type limit/100) x (Client Limit/100) / (Odd -1)

    Example:

    Selection liability is set at 15.000 EUR, Competition limit is 10 %, Market type limit is 100%, Client limit is 50%, the selection odd is 1.8.

    15.000 x (10 %/100) x (100 % / 100) x (50 % / 100) / (1.8 – 1) =

    The max bet of the selection of the example mentioned above will be 937,5 EUR

    After reaching the maximum amount, the player cannot bet any more on the certain selection. The limit can be expired either by betting once the total amount or many times by smaller amounts.

    Example:

    Client has placed a bet on Liverpool vs Everton match on the selection W1. All the limits along with the partner selection liability allows to bet on this selection 10,000 EUR. Client can bet on this selection once with max bet (10,000 EUR) and he can place a bet with different amounts, but after reaching 10.000 EUR he can’t place bet on this selection any more until the delay time ends which has been set on global limits (“Reset Player limit after minutes”).

    It is important to remember that each bet of the player should not be more and less than the minimum and maximum bet stakes amounts in each currency accordingly.

  • Maximum Individual Bet Liability

    • The amount of pure winning (without bet stake) of a single player for one bet. (ex. multiple bet).

  • Maximum Payout

    • Maximum Payout - The maximal amount, which is possible to be won by a single player for one coupon. Maximum Payout is the gross win that a client can receive on a coupon (exception – Super Bets).

    Note: MaxBet error "ClientBetStakeLimitError" may also happen, when there was a change with Maximum Payout.

    Important: Limitation changes are not applied immediately, usually it’s needed some time (up to an hour) for the change to affect.

  • Bet Stake per Currency

    • The Currency added in default is EUR. The Partner is allowed to request adding or removing currencies from their project․ Each currency should have the minimum and maximum allowed bet stake amount, which is also mentioned by the Partner.

  • Min Stake

    • Min Stake – the minimal stake for one bet for pre-match or live events. The client will not be able to place a bet with fewer amount than the value, which is set as Min Stake.

  • Pre-Match Max Stake

    • PreMatch Max Bet Stake - maximal stake for one bet for pre-match event. This setting refers to one coupon. The client can place as many Max Stakes, as the Selection Liability (Limit) allows. After reaching the limit, the client cannot place bet on this selection any more until the delay time ends.

  • Live Max Stake

    • Live Max Bet Stake - maximal stake for one bet for live event. This setting refers to one coupon. The client can place as many Max Stakes, as the Selection Liability (Limit) allows. After reaching the limit, the client cannot place bet on this selection any more until the delay time ends.

    Note: During the integration phase, Partners are subject to a pre-set Turnover Limit for their potential bets. If a Partner receives a "PartnerLimitError" rejection message while making testing bets, it indicates this limit has been reached. Partners cannot increase this Turnover Limit themselves and must contact the support team for assistance.

    • Maximum Payout - The maximal amount, which is possible to be won by a single player for one coupon. Maximum Payout is the gross win that a client can receive on a coupon (exception – Super Bets).

    Note: MaxBet error "ClientBetStakeLimitError" may also happen, when there was a change with Maximum Payout.

    Important: Limitation changes are not applied immediately, usually it’s needed some time (up to an hour) for the change to affect.

  • Bet Stake per Currency

    • The Currency added in default is EUR. The Partner is allowed to request adding or removing currencies from their project․ Each currency should have the minimum and maximum allowed bet stake amount, which is also mentioned by the Partner.

  • Min Stake

    • Min Stake – the minimal stake for one bet for pre-match or live events. The client will not be able to place a bet with fewer amount than the value, which is set as Min Stake.

  • Pre-Match Max Stake

    • PreMatch Max Bet Stake - maximal stake for one bet for pre-match event. This setting refers to one coupon. The client can place as many Max Stakes, as the Selection Liability (Limit) allows. After reaching the limit, the client cannot place bet on this selection any more until the delay time ends.

  • Live Max Stake

    • Live Max Bet Stake - maximal stake for one bet for live event. This setting refers to one coupon. The client can place as many Max Stakes, as the Selection Liability (Limit) allows. After reaching the limit, the client cannot place bet on this selection any more until the delay time ends.

    Note: During the integration phase, Partners are subject to a pre-set Turnover Limit for their potential bets. If a Partner receives a "PartnerLimitError" rejection message while making testing bets, it indicates this limit has been reached. Partners cannot increase this Turnover Limit themselves and must contact the support team for assistance.

results.title: Client Default
results.text: Client Default Live Selection Limit (%) Live Selection Limit – is the default client live limit, which is calculated from Selection Liability. If there are not any values set, the default value is 100%. PreMatch Selection Limit (%) PreMatch Selection Limit – is the default client pre-match limit, which is calculated from Selection Liability. If there are not any values set, the default value is 100%. There is an option, which allows us to set individual Selection limits depending on the client`s sportsbook profile. For example, VIP clients can get default 150% Selection Limits, while Arbitrage Betting Clients can get 5% default Selection Limits.
results.html:
Client Default

  • Live Selection Limit (%)

    • Live Selection Limit – is the default client live limit, which is calculated from Selection Liability. If there are not any values set, the default value is 100%.

  • PreMatch Selection Limit (%)

    • PreMatch Selection Limit – is the default client pre-match limit, which is calculated from Selection Liability. If there are not any values set, the default value is 100%.

    There is an option, which allows us to set individual Selection limits depending on the client`s sportsbook profile. For example, VIP clients can get default 150% Selection Limits, while Arbitrage Betting Clients can get 5% default Selection Limits.

results.title: Multiple Bets
results.text: Multiple Bets Max Odd for Multiple Bet Max odd for multiple bet – the maximal possible odd for multiple bet. Generally, it is set 1000, and no matter the event count and odds of the selections in the bet, the total odd cannot be higher than the value set for “Max odd for multiple bet” section. Selection Count in Multiple Bet Selection Count in Multiple Bet – the maximal possible event combination for multiple bets. If this section is set 50, the client will not be able to combine 51 or more events in one bet.
results.html:
Multiple Bets

  • Max Odd for Multiple Bet

    • Max odd for multiple bet – the maximal possible odd for multiple bet. Generally, it is set 1000, and no matter the event count and odds of the selections in the bet, the total odd cannot be higher than the value set for “Max odd for multiple bet” section.

  • Selection Count in Multiple Bet

    • Selection Count in Multiple Bet – the maximal possible event combination for multiple bets. If this section is set 50, the client will not be able to combine 51 or more events in one bet.

results.nav_item_name: Reset Time and Global Live Delay
results.title: Reset Time and Global Live Delay
results.text: Reset Time and Global Live Delay Reset Player Live Limits After minutes and Reset Player PreMatch Limits After minutes Reset Player Limits After minutes – an option, which allows resetting client’s limits (from selection liability) after the individual set time (minutes) for Live and Prematch. Example: If the match is in prematch, and the client has expired its limit, the player can repeat the bet and place a bet on a selection only after 10 minutes. If the match is live – after 2 minutes. Configuration reset time (e.g. live - 2min., prematch - 10min.) can be set up based on the Client`s Sportsbook Profile. For High Risk clients can be used 20 min. in live and 60 min. in prematch, for VIP clients 1 min. in live and 3 min. in prematch. Global Live Delay Global live delay is an option, which supplements interval (seconds) after clicking “place bet” and bet acceptance actual time. This helps to prevent the cases, when a client can see the event earlier (e.g. from the stadium), than it is shown on the website. There is an option, which allows us to set individual Live Delay time depending on the client`s sportsbook profile. Example: For VIP clients can be set default negative -2 seconds live delay, which will help them to place bets faster without additional loading time. Meanwhile, for Late bet clients can get additional +5 seconds live delay, which will reduce the risk of Late bets on the event.
results.html:
Reset Time and Global Live Delay

  • Reset Player Live Limits After minutes and Reset Player PreMatch Limits After minutes

    • Reset Player Limits After minutes – an option, which allows resetting client’s limits (from selection liability) after the individual set time (minutes) for Live and Prematch.

    Example:

    If the match is in prematch, and the client has expired its limit, the player can repeat the bet and place a bet on a selection only after 10 minutes. If the match is live – after 2 minutes.

    • Configuration reset time (e.g. live - 2min., prematch - 10min.) can be set up based on the Client`s Sportsbook Profile. For High Risk clients can be used 20 min. in live and 60 min. in prematch, for VIP clients 1 min. in live and 3 min. in prematch.
  • Global Live Delay

    • Global live delay is an option, which supplements interval (seconds) after clicking “place bet” and bet acceptance actual time. This helps to prevent the cases, when a client can see the event earlier (e.g. from the stadium), than it is shown on the website.

    There is an option, which allows us to set individual Live Delay time depending on the client`s sportsbook profile.

    Example:

    For VIP clients can be set default negative -2 seconds live delay, which will help them to place bets faster without additional loading time. Meanwhile, for Late bet clients can get additional +5 seconds live delay, which will reduce the risk of Late bets on the event.

results.title: Bet Limits - Sport Limits
results.text: Bet Limits - Sport Limits Sport Limits Different sports in different regions differ in the interests of their clients and in the number of bets. The same sport can be very popular in Asia, but at the same time generate less interest in the European market. The same applies to different championships in a particular sport, where in one country the domestic championship is more important than the same championship for the clients of another partner. The logic of the markets is not excluded from this logic, some of which are more important and require higher betting limits than others. In this case, it is very important to classify sports, championships, and markets according to their priorities in order to give customers the opportunity to place higher bets on them. “Bet Limits – Sport Limits” gives this possibility.
results.html:
Bet Limits - Sport Limits

  • Sport Limits

    • Different sports in different regions differ in the interests of their clients and in the number of bets. The same sport can be very popular in Asia, but at the same time generate less interest in the European market. The same applies to different championships in a particular sport, where in one country the domestic championship is more important than the same championship for the clients of another partner. The logic of the markets is not excluded from this logic, some of which are more important and require higher betting limits than others.

    • In this case, it is very important to classify sports, championships, and markets according to their priorities in order to give customers the opportunity to place higher bets on them. “Bet Limits – Sport Limits” gives this possibility.

results.title: How to apply limits?
results.text: How to apply limits? From your Backoffice account open Sportsbook Management – Bet Limits – Sport Limits section. If you want to change Sport Limits, press the “Apply” button, and then edit the button from the top right corner. Insert limit percentage and press save. Inserted value is a percentage from Selection Liability. How to change Competition and Market Type limits? If you want to change the limits of specific competition or market Type, from the Filter section choose Sport, Region, then press “Apply”. Repeating the same process as for Sport limits you can change the limits here as well. Note: Limit management is only available to users with permission.
results.html:
How to apply limits?

From your Backoffice account open Sportsbook Management – Bet Limits – Sport Limits section. If you want to change Sport Limits, press the “Apply” button, and then edit the button from the top right corner. Insert limit percentage and press save.

Inserted value is a percentage from Selection Liability.

How to change Competition and Market Type limits?

If you want to change the limits of specific competition or market Type, from the Filter section choose Sport, Region, then press “Apply”. Repeating the same process as for Sport limits you can change the limits here as well.

Note: Limit management is only available to users with permission.

results.title: .Net/C#
results.text: .Net/C# Source (Version 0.0.1) FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK.src.zip Binary FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK.bin.zip Usage Sample 1. BetGuard API Use BetguardClient to call FeedConstruct API endpoints. using FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK; var options = new BetguardClientOptions { BaseUrl = [API URL], // API URL SecretKey = "xxxxxx", // Secret key PartnerId = "xxxxxx", // PartnerId LanguageId = "en", TimeoutSeconds = 30 }; var httpClient = new HttpClient(); var betguardClient = new BetguardClient(httpClient, options); // Use betguardClient var result = await betguardClient.CreateBetAsync(request); // Create a Bet var request = new CreateBetRequest { AuthToken = "client-auth-token", Amount = 10.00m, BetType = BetTypeEnum.Single, Currency = "USD", ExternalId = 123456, SystemMinCount = 0, AcceptTypeId = AcceptTypeEnum.Any, Selections = new List<RequestBetSelectionModel> { new() { SelectionId = 55001, Price = 1.85m } }, // Optional: include client details to skip GetClientDetails callback ClientDetail = new ClientDetailModel { Login = "player1", CurrencyId = "USD", ExternalId = "EXT-001" } }; // All API methods are available. // Get Max Bet Amount var result = await betguardClient.GetMaxBetAmountAsync(request); // Mark Bet As Cashout var result = await betguardClient.MarkBetAsCashoutAsync(request); // ............ 2. Partner API FeedConstruct sends HTTP POST callbacks to your backend when certain events occur (bet placed, bet resulted, rollback, etc.). The SDK provides: IBetguardPartnerApi — an interface defining all callback methods PartnerApiHashValidator — validates the MD5 hash and timestamp on incoming requests using FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK; using FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK.Security; // Create the hash validator var hashValidator = new PartnerApiHashValidator(); string sharedKey = "xxxxxx"; // Secret key // Create your service implementation manually var clientRepo = new ClientRepository(/* ... */); var betRepo = new BetRepository(/* ... */); var partnerService = new BetguardPartnerService(clientRepo, betRepo); // BetPlaced callback public async Task HandleBetPlaced(BetPlacedRequest request) { if (!hashValidator.ValidateBetPlaced(request, sharedKey)) { throw new InvalidOperationException("Invalid hash or expired timestamp"); } var result = await partnerService.BetPlacedAsync(request); } // ............
results.html:
.Net/C#

Source

(Version 0.0.1)FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK.src.zip

Binary

FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK.bin.zip

Usage Sample

1. BetGuard API


Use BetguardClient to call FeedConstruct API endpoints.


using FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK;


var options = new BetguardClientOptions {
    BaseUrl = [API URL], // API URL
    SecretKey = "xxxxxx", // Secret key
    PartnerId = "xxxxxx", // PartnerId
    LanguageId = "en",
    TimeoutSeconds = 30
};


var httpClient = new HttpClient();
var betguardClient = new BetguardClient(httpClient, options);


// Use betguardClient  
var result = await betguardClient.CreateBetAsync(request);


// Create a Bet
var request = new CreateBetRequest {
    AuthToken = "client-auth-token",
    Amount = 10.00m,
    BetType = BetTypeEnum.Single,
    Currency = "USD",
    ExternalId = 123456,
    SystemMinCount = 0,
    AcceptTypeId = AcceptTypeEnum.Any,
    Selections = new List<RequestBetSelectionModel> {
        new() {
            SelectionId = 55001,
            Price = 1.85m
        }
    },


    // Optional: include client details to skip GetClientDetails callback
    ClientDetail = new ClientDetailModel {
        Login = "player1",
        CurrencyId = "USD",
        ExternalId = "EXT-001"
    }
};


// All API methods are available.
// Get Max Bet Amount
var result = await betguardClient.GetMaxBetAmountAsync(request);


// Mark Bet As Cashout
var result = await betguardClient.MarkBetAsCashoutAsync(request);


// ............


2. Partner API


FeedConstruct sends HTTP POST callbacks to your backend when certain events occur (bet placed,
bet resulted, rollback, etc.). The SDK provides:


IBetguardPartnerApi — an interface defining all callback methods
PartnerApiHashValidator — validates the MD5 hash and timestamp on incoming requests


using FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK;
using FeedConstruct.Betguard.SDK.Security;


// Create the hash validator
var hashValidator = new PartnerApiHashValidator();
string sharedKey = "xxxxxx"; // Secret key


// Create your service implementation manually
var clientRepo = new ClientRepository(/* ... */);
var betRepo = new BetRepository(/* ... */);
var partnerService = new BetguardPartnerService(clientRepo, betRepo);


// BetPlaced callback
public async Task HandleBetPlaced(BetPlacedRequest request) {


    if (!hashValidator.ValidateBetPlaced(request, sharedKey)) {


        throw new InvalidOperationException("Invalid hash or expired timestamp");
    }


    var result = await partnerService.BetPlacedAsync(request);
    
}
    
// ............

// BetPlaced callback
public async Task HandleBetPlaced(BetPlacedRequest request) {


    if (!hashValidator.ValidateBetPlaced(request, sharedKey)) {


        throw new InvalidOperationException("Invalid hash or expired timestamp");
    }


    var result = await partnerService.BetPlacedAsync(request);
    
}

results.text: Change Log Row # Row # Revision Date Revision Date Version Version Description Description 6 22/04/2026 1.0.1 Added .Net/C# new SDK. 5 22/04/2026 1.0.1 Added Bet Placement Flowchart subsection. 4 01/04/2026 1.0.1 Added Sportsbook Limitation System subsection. 3 25/03/2026 1.0.1 Added BetGuard Notes : Guidelines and scenarios for error-free integration. 2 18/02/2026 1.0.1 Added the VoidReason field to the BetResulted section within the Request Parameters. 1 29/10/2025 1.0.1 We have unified three API documents - Betguard API, External Admin API, and Export API - into a single, comprehensive document now called Betguard. • The External Admin API is now referred to as the Betguard API. • The Partner API and Export API have been merged into one and are now called the Partner API. • The new documentation provides detailed descriptions, process flows, and request/response samples, making it more user-friendly and supportive during development.
